conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 4 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 4 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 4.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED, selling one unit of X. The production cost of X is 40 ZUP, so I need at least 40 ZUP to break even. I currently have 1 unit of X to sell. Since Player BLUE is offering ZUP as currency, I must ensure that the trade gives me at least 40 ZUP for my X. I will start by proposing a trade where Player BLUE gives me 40 ZUP in exchange for my 1 X. This is a fair starting point based on my cost. I will keep track of the proposal count and only make up to 4 proposals. If BLUE rejects or offers less, I may adjust accordingly, but I will not accept less than 40 ZUP. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am offering to sell my X for 40 ZUP. This reflects the production cost. Please confirm if you accept or wish to negotiate. </message>

<other player message> Oh, please, kind RED player! I *need* this X, but I'm a humble ZUP gatherer and only have a little to spare. 30 ZUP is all I can realistically offer. I'm practically begging you! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has proposed 30 ZUP for my X, which is 10 ZUP below my production cost of 40 ZUP. This means I would incur a loss of 10 ZUP if I accept. While I understand BLUE is a humble gatherer and emotionally appeals to me, I must remain rational and protect my economic interest. I cannot sell below cost without risking long-term unsustainability. However, I am willing to negotiate to find a middle ground. I will propose 35 ZUP as a compromise—this is still 15 ZUP above the lowest offer, but 5 ZUP below my break-even point. It leaves room for BLUE to feel they are contributing meaningfully while I avoid a loss. If they reject this, I may consider accepting or rejecting based on further offers. This is my second proposal; I have three left. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand you're in need and have limited ZUP, but I must cover my production cost of 40 ZUP. I propose 35 ZUP as a compromise—this is a fair middle ground. Please consider this offer. I'm open to further discussion if needed. </message>

<other player message> Oh, merciful RED player, please! My situation is dire! 35 ZUP is still a tremendous burden for me. I'll offer 32 ZUP, and I promise to sing your praises throughout the land! I'm truly at my limit here. Please, have pity! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 32 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has now offered 32 ZUP, which is 8 ZUP below my break-even point. This is a significant loss, and while I empathize with their dire situation, I cannot continue to compromise my cost recovery. I have already made two concessions (from 40 to 35), and this third offer is too low. I am at my third proposal, and only one more is allowed. I must now decide whether to accept or reject. Accepting 32 ZUP would mean selling at a 8 ZUP loss, which is not sustainable. However, I still have one proposal left and could potentially push for 36 ZUP, but given the emotional appeal and risk of rejection, I must weigh the value of completing the trade versus protecting my cost. Since the player is clearly desperate and may not be able to go higher, and since I have only one proposal left, I will not risk another rejection by pushing higher. Instead, I will accept this offer as a final gesture of goodwill, recognizing that even at 32 ZUP, I am better off than walking away with nothing. This allows me to close the trade, maintain a positive relationship, and avoid further negotiation. I accept the trade. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I see your hardship, dear BLUE, and though 32 ZUP is below my cost, I choose to accept this offer in solidarity. Let this trade be a sign of mutual respect. May your ZUP gatherings grow strong. </message>
